title: The Baby Shower

jokes.text: They tease you with, 'You may have already won.' I'd like once for a sweepstakes company to have some guts. Just tell people the truth one time. Send out envelopes: 'You have definitely lost.' You turn it over, giant printing: 'Not even close.' You open it, there's a letter of explanation: 'Even we cannot believe how badly you've done in this contest.'
jokes.setup: Common frustration with sweepstakes marketing language
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of increasingly brutal rejection messages
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational comedy with perfect escalation. Takes a relatable annoyance and pushes it to absurd extremes with great specificity.

jokes.text: Don't get up. I'd like to help, but my neck....
jokes.setup: Someone struggling to move something heavy
jokes.punchline: George's weak excuse to avoid helping
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures George's lazy, excuse-making character with believable specificity.

jokes.text: Students can't clean. It's anathema. They don't like it. How long you been waiting to squeeze that into a conversation?
jokes.setup: George using the word 'anathema' in casual conversation
jokes.punchline: Jerry calling out George's obvious vocabulary showing off
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-comedy moment that feels natural to these characters while being very quotable.

jokes.text: It was the single most damaging experience of my life. Aside from seeing my father naked.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing breaking a statue as traumatic
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of an even worse trauma
jokes.explanation: Perfect throwaway line with great economy and unexpected dark turn.

jokes.text: Why don't you just settle it like mature adults? -Potato man. -No, no. No potato man. Inka-dink.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting they settle dispute maturely
jokes.punchline: Immediate regression to childish selection methods
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd moment showing how these adults never quite grew up, with great comedic timing.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. No. -...it -What? What are you doing? No, no. Oh, okay. All right. He's out, I get it. -No, no, no. I'm it. I win. -No. He's it. He wins. It is good.
jokes.punchline: Complete confusion over childish game rules
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of adults completely screwing up a kids' game, building on previous setup.

jokes.text: Hey, I look like Joe Friday in Dragnet.
jokes.punchline: Visual comparison to old TV detective
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that relies on costume and pop culture reference without much craft.

jokes.text: The inka-dink. You were it. -It's bad? -It's very bad.
jokes.punchline: Jerry admitting he cheated George
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that adds moral complexity to the childish game, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: Or maybe he's dead.
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion of death as explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan dark humor delivered with casual indifference, very economical.

jokes.text: Greetings and salutations. I beg your forgiveness. My tardiness was unavoidable. Rava, my love. Elaine, dear friend. And you must be Jerry, lord of the manor. My liege. A pleasure to serve you.
jokes.punchline: Overly flowery, pretentious greeting style
jokes.explanation: Establishes Ray's pretentious character perfectly, though a bit verbose for the payoff.

jokes.text: Your palace shall sparkle like the stars in heaven upon your safe arrival, sire. The toilet brush is under the sink.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's blunt practical instruction
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Ray's pretension and Jerry's pragmatism, excellent juxtaposition.

jokes.text: I don't feel comfortable with a maid either. There's that guilt when someone's cleaning your house. You're sitting on the sofa, they go by with the vacuum. 'I'm really sorry about this. I don't know why I left that stuff over there.'
jokes.setup: General discomfort with having cleaning help
jokes.punchline: Specific awkward internal monologue
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about relatable awkward situation, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: That's why I could never be a maid. I would have that attitude. I'd find them, wherever they are. 'I suppose you couldn't do this. No, don't get up. Let me clean up your filth. No, you couldn't dust. Oh, this is too tough, isn't it?'
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why he couldn't be a maid
jokes.punchline: Increasingly aggressive passive-aggressive dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous setup, showing Jerry's neurotic personality perfectly.

jokes.text: He uncoagulated the top of the dishwashing liquid.
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'uncoagulated' for cleaning detail
jokes.explanation: Great made-up word that perfectly describes something we all recognize, very Seinfeld-esque.

jokes.text: He must be like Rubber Man. There's no Rubber Man. Why did I think there was a Rubber Man? There's Elastic Man, Plastic Man....
jokes.setup: Jerry wondering how Ray cleaned tight space
jokes.punchline: Confusion about superhero names
jokes.explanation: Amusing tangent about comic book knowledge, though not particularly deep.

jokes.text: Elaine, he Windexed the little peephole.
jokes.setup: Continuing to marvel at Ray's thorough cleaning
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific cleaning detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail that shows obsessive cleaning thoroughness, economical and specific.

jokes.text: Shouldn't you be out on a ledge somewhere?
jokes.punchline: Jerry suggesting he should be suicidal
jokes.explanation: Dark, cutting response to pretentious behavior, very quotable and economical.

jokes.text: Jerry. -What? Ray, would you give me a hand, please? Yeah, I'm coming.
jokes.punchline: Jerry completely spacing out, awkward pause
jokes.explanation: Simple beat showing Jerry's distraction, functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Sweet elixir, its fragrant nectar a soothing balm for the soul.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously flowery description of tea
jokes.explanation: Consistent character moment showing Ray's pretentious nature, though predictable.

jokes.text: My mother left us when I was 6 years old. All seven of us. We never heard from her again. I hope she's rotting in an alley somewhere.
jokes.punchline: Ray's shockingly dark overshare about abandonment
jokes.explanation: Shocking tonal shift from casual to deeply dark, perfectly uncomfortable and well-structured.

jokes.text: My mom's down in Florida. She's got one of those condos. Hot down there in the summer. You ever been down there?
jokes.setup: Ray's dark confession about his mother
jokes.punchline: Jerry's painfully awkward attempt at normal conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy showing Jerry's desperate attempt to normalize after Ray's overshare.

jokes.text: In Scandinavian mythology, the pastries were the food of the gods.
jokes.punchline: Ray's ridiculous mythological reference
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Ray moment, showing his pretentious pseudo-intellectual character.

jokes.text: Dinner's for suckers.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's bizarre philosophical stance on dinner
jokes.explanation: Incredibly quotable and absurd line with perfect economy, became a minor Seinfeld catchphrase.

jokes.text: She's from Finland, for crying out loud. Finland! Do you understand? I know Finland. They're neutral.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's confused response about Finland's neutrality
jokes.explanation: Absurd tangent about geopolitics in relationship context, amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: 'I find you so charming.' You wuss. -Did you call me a wuss? -What did you say? I said, 'luss.' I'm at a 'luss.'
jokes.setup: George insulting Ray under his breath
jokes.punchline: Ray mishearing and George's weak cover-up
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay and escalation showing George's cowardice when confronted.

jokes.text: That's it! I can't take it anymore! You stole the statue! You're a thief! You're a liar! -George. -Who is this? I'm the judge and the jury, pal. And the verdict is, guilty!
jokes.setup: George's building frustration with Ray's excuses
jokes.punchline: George's explosive courtroom-style outburst
jokes.explanation: Perfect George explosion moment, showing his neurotic inability to let things go.

jokes.text: I called the registrar's office. I checked you out. They have no record of a Ray Thomas at that school, you liar! That is because I am registered under my full legal name Raymond Thomas Wochinski. Ray Thomas is my professional name. You mean 'alias.'
jokes.setup: George's gotcha moment about Ray's school records
jokes.punchline: Ray having a reasonable explanation, George pushing further
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing George's paranoia even when proven wrong.

jokes.text: How did you get the goop out of the top of the dishwashing liquid? It was like a brand-new nozzle.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's completely off-topic practical question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - practical concerns trumping dramatic confrontation, great callback.

jokes.text: We made love on the floor like two animals. Ray is insatiable.
jokes.setup: Casual question about going out last night
jokes.punchline: Graphic overshare about sex life
jokes.explanation: Effective shock humor through inappropriate oversharing, though not particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: Was Jerry? I can't remember.
jokes.punchline: Elaine's brutal assessment of Jerry's performance
jokes.explanation: Devastating deadpan insult with perfect economy, cuts deep with minimal words.

jokes.text: There are no small coincidences and big coincidences. No. There are degrees of coincidences. No. There are only coincidences. Ask anyone.
jokes.punchline: Philosophical debate about types of coincidences
jokes.explanation: Typical Seinfeld absurdist debate about meaningless distinctions, though drags slightly.

jokes.text: She doesn't react to disappointment very well. Unlike me. I'm not happy about this.
jokes.punchline: Claiming he handles disappointment well while clearly not
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jerry's self-awareness and lack thereof simultaneously.

jokes.text: Why don't we just throw a Molotov cocktail through their window.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what to do about Ray
jokes.punchline: George's violent overreaction suggestion
jokes.explanation: Typical George overreaction, showing his tendency toward extreme solutions.

jokes.text: Yeah, he'll have my parents.
jokes.setup: George suggesting Ray will face justice in the afterlife
jokes.punchline: Jerry suggesting his parents as punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark callback to earlier parent disappointment, shows Jerry's relationship with them.

jokes.text: You just make love to that wall, pervert.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual command during fake arrest
jokes.explanation: Pure Kramer absurdity in his fake cop performance, character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm a cop. I'm a good cop. I'm a damn good cop!
jokes.setup: Ray questioning if Kramer is really a cop
jokes.punchline: Kramer's increasingly emphatic affirmation
jokes.explanation: Kramer committing to the bit with characteristic intensity, though predictable.

jokes.text: Well, let's put it this way: I didn't take him to People's Court.
jokes.setup: Asked what he did to Ray
jokes.punchline: Implying violence by contrasting with TV court show
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection implying violence through pop culture reference, very quotable.

jokes.text: You go to the beach, go in the water. Put your wallet in the sneaker. Who's gonna know? What criminal mind could penetrate this fortress of security?
jokes.punchline: Mocking the obvious inadequacy of hiding wallet in shoe
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational closer, relatable with good sarcastic delivery.

jokes.text: 'I tied a bow. They can't get through that.' 'I put the wallet down by the toe of the sneaker. They never look there. They check the heel, they move on.'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous security logic
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the setup, though not as strong as the initial premise.

tmdb_overview: Elaine hosts a shower in Jerry's apartment while he's away. An illegal cable hookup and unexpected returns create chaos.
